items.title: Node
items.description: The JavaScript runtime for the server. While I like experimenting with alternatives these days, it's rare to avoid Node in today's web development.

items.title: Bun
items.description: Fast JavaScript runtime & bundler with TypeScript as a first-class citizen. I've enjoyed playing with Bun a lot and believe in its potential, so it's now my go-to for private projects and prototypes.

items.title: Express
items.description: A minimal, unopinionated web framework for Node. It's a rather unexciting choice, but it serves its purpose well.

items.title: Swagger
items.description: A toolset for designing and documenting REST APIs. Integrating OpenAPI/Swagger into a backend is almost a must-have, in my opinion.

items.title: SQL Server
items.description: Microsoft's enterprise relational database. While I'm not a SQL wizard, I'm comfortable working with a full-scale DBMS.

items.title: SQLite
items.description: A lightweight, file-based SQL database. My favorite for prototypes and private projects; it's almost magic how performant and reliable a file-based database can be.
